# About Me

## Who I Am
I'm Namare Shakib Angkon, a dedicated Computer Science and Engineering student at United International University. My expertise bridges the gap between software and hardware. I specialize in building AI-driven applications (leveraging LLMs, NLP, and real-time object detection), crafting accessible full-stack web platforms, and engineering embedded systems like portable hardware solutions.

## What Drives Me
My work spans a diverse set of technical domains:

## What Drives Me
My work spans a diverse set of technical domains:

**Machine Learning & AI**: I'm deeply passionate about applying Artificial Intelligence to solve real-world problems. Whether it is building ARGUS, an on-device AI navigation system for the visually impaired, or implementing intelligent risk-flag generation for healthcare platforms, I focus on systems that make a tangible impact.
heading: What Drives Me

text:
building ARGUS, an on-device AI navigation system for the visually impaired, or implementing intelligent risk-flag generation for healthcare platforms, I focus on systems that make a tangible impact.

**Embedded Systems & Hardware**: I don't just write software; I enjoy building the hardware that runs it. Projects like Portable Braille—a low-cost printer that embosses digital text onto A4 paper using Raspberry Pi, stepper motors, and 3D printing—highlight my ability to combine physical engineering with code.

**Full-Stack Engineering**: I enjoy crafting complete, accessible platforms. Through projects like ShareStrength and Maternify, I've designed and developed web applications that empower users, particularly those with disabilities or unique healthcare needs.

essible platforms. Through projects like ShareStrength and Maternify, I've designed and developed web applications that empower users, particularly those with disabilities or unique healthcare needs. 

## My Approach
I believe in:
- **Accessibility & Impact**: Technology is most powerful when it empowers those who need it most.
- **Hardware-Software Synergy**: The best solutions often require understanding both the code and the physical devices it runs on.
- **Continuous Learning**: From winning hackathons to securing Pre-Seed funding, I'm always looking for the next challenge to learn and grow.
heading: My Approach

text:
# Projects

## ARGUS — AI-Powered Smart Glasses for the Visually Impaired

### Overview
ARGUS is an on-device AI navigation system designed to empower visually impaired individuals by integrating real-time object detection, depth estimation, and voice-command NLU on a Jetson Orin Nano.

### Technical Details
- **Technologies**: Python, YOLOv8, TensorRT, Whisper
- **Hardware**: Jetson Orin Nano
- **Key Features**: 
  - Real-time object detection to navigate surroundings
  - Depth estimation for obstacle avoidance
  - Voice-command NLU (Natural Language Understanding) for interactive assistance

---

## Portable Braille — Making Print Accessible

### Overview
Engineered a low-cost, portable Braille printer that embosses digital text onto A4 paper using stepper motors, solenoid actuators, and IR sensors.
heading: Portable Braille — Making Print Accessible

text:
table Braille — Making Print Accessible

### Overview
Engineered a low-cost, portable Braille printer that embosses digital text onto A4 paper using stepper motors, solenoid actuators, and IR sensors.

### Technical Details
- **Technologies**: Raspberry Pi, Python, OCR (Optical Character Recognition), 3D Printing
- **Hardware**: Stepper motors, solenoid actuators, IR sensors
- **Key Features**: 
  - Converts digital text into physical Braille
  - Low-cost and portable form factor
  - Uses 3D printed components for accessibility and customizability
- **Repository**: Available on GitHub (https://github.com/namaray/Portable-Braille-Printer)

---

## ShareStrength — Empowering Individuals with Disabilities

### Overview
Built a web platform connecting people with disabilities to trusted helpers using AI-based recommendations and accessibility tools.

### Technical Details
- **Technologies**: PHP, MySQL, JavaScript, Tailwind CSS
- **Features**:
  - Secure and accessible user interface
  - AI-based recommendations to match users with helpers
  - Integrated accessibility features tailored for diverse needs
- **Repository**: Available on GitHub (https://github.com/namaray/ShareStrength)

---

## Maternify — Prenatal & Postnatal Health Tracker
heading: ShareStrength — Empowering Individuals with Disabilities

- **Repository**: Available on GitHub (https://github.com/namaray/ShareStrength)

---

## Maternify — Prenatal & Postnatal Health Tracker

### Overview
A prenatal/postnatal symptom tracking platform with intelligent risk-flag generation to help expecting and new mothers monitor their health. I served as the main frontend designer for this project.

### Technical Details
- **Technologies**: Python, AI APIs, React
- **Features**:
  - Intuitive and calming frontend design
  - Symptom tracking tailored to different pregnancy stages
  - Intelligent risk-flag generation leveraging AI APIs

---
